notes: With-skill: 94% pass rate (17/18) vs without-skill: 67% (12/18) — +27% improvement
One 'failure' in with-skill (Test 3 parent task) was actually correct behavior — the assertion was misaligned with the test scenario
Biggest skill advantage: Test 2 research pipeline — skill prevents premature guide creation; baseline creates guide before user approval
Test 3 session-end: skill produces structured logs, proper task movement with dates, and complete CLAUDE.md updates; baseline loses structure
With-skill uses ~45% more tokens and ~2x more time due to reading reference files and following structured protocols
Token/time overhead is justified: prevents costly rework in multi-session projects (wrong guide, lost task structure, missing dates)
